findings.observation: PDP buying area is plain text ('Buy online or find it in the candy aisle…') with no price, add-to-cart, or structured retailer module where the eye lands.
Where To Buy shows an error banner ('There was something wrong!') and blog cards — no store locator, retailer list, or online-buying cards.
Homepage leads with one product focus and a single 'WHERE TO BUY' route; no mission cards for the distinct buyer jobs (relief vs candy vs cooking).
All-products grid lists many SKUs by product name with no need-first grouping; shoppers must decode product names rather than shop a job.
GIN GINS category presents many flavors with no multi-flavor sampler or trial entry point.
Ginger Rescue formats are shown separately; no travel/motion-sickness kit bundles the lozenge + tablet formats.
GLP-1 article educates (nausea affects up to 50% of GLP-1 patients) but offers no packaged, reorderable ginger+turmeric routine.
Consumable PDP has no 'add to routine' / reorder-reminder path for repeat usage.
Health-content layout (education + formats) exists and could anchor condition landing pages like morning sickness.
